revision.comment: Reverted 1 edit by [[Special:Contributions/223.24.168.144|223.24.168.144]] ([[User talk:223.24.168.144|talk]]) to last revision by LOOKSQUARE
revision.text: {{For|the style guideline about using colon in articles|MOS:COLON}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:COLON|WP:COLONTRICK|H::}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''colon trick''' is a method of providing a link to a [[Wikipedia:Category|category]], [[Help:Images|image]] or [[Help:Interwiki linking|interwiki link]] without adding the page to the category, displaying the image or adding the interwiki link to the [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage links]]. It also allows for linking to pages with titles beginning with a recognized external link prefix, such as [[:http://]] or [[://Hus]]. By adding a [[Colon (punctuation)|colon]] just inside the opening double square brackets of the link, the default behaviour of the [[MediaWiki software]] will be bypassed. It will appear as a conventional [[Help:Link|wikilink]]. ==Examples== * [[Category:Help]] would list this page in Category:Help, but [[:Category:Help]] will produce a link to [[:Category:Help]]. * [[File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] would display an image, but [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] will produce a link to [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]]. * [[pt:Internet]] will create an [[interlanguage link]], but [[:pt:Internet]] will produce an interwiki link to [[:pt:Internet]] (in the Portuguese Wikipedia). * [[//Hus]] will produce an (invalid) external link wrapped in brackets ("[[//Hus]]"), but [[://Hus]] will produce a link to [[://Hus]]. * [[Computer]] links to the [[Computer]] article; adding a colon like [[:Computer]] can be done, but the colon is unneeded and makes no difference. ==With templates== A colon can also be used to transclude a page in mainspace, rather than template space. For example, {{tlx|The Beatles}} will transclude [[Template:The Beatles]], but {{[[The Beatles|:The Beatles]]}} will transclude the mainspace article [[The Beatles]]. This is useful when the mainspace article has been specially formatted to be transcluded in other articles, by using <noinclude> or <includeonly> tags to indicate the portion that should be included when the article is transcluded. ==With edit summaries== Normally, linking to a [[Wikipedia:User pages|user page]] in an [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] sends a notification to the user. A colon can be used to escape this behaviour. For example, the edit summary "corrected typo by [[User:Example]]" will ping [[User:Example]], but "corrected typo by [[:User:Example]]" will not. {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia link help|Colon trick]]

revision.text: {{For|the style guideline about using colon in articles|MOS:COLON}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:COLON|WP:COLONTRICK|H::}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''colon trick''' is a method of providing a link to a [[Wikipedia:Category|category]], [[Help:Images|image]] or [[Help:Interwiki linking|interwiki link]] without adding the page to the category, displaying the image or adding the interwiki link to the [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage links]]. It also allows for linking to pages with titles beginning with a recognized external link prefix, such as [[:http://]] or [[://Hus]]. By adding a [[Colon (punctuation)|colon]] just inside the opening double square brackets of the link, the default behaviour of the [[MediaWiki software]] will be bypassed. It will appear as a conventional [[Help:Link|wikilink]]. ==Examples== * [[Category:Help]] would list this page in Category:Help, but [[:Category:Help]] will produce a link to [[:Category:Help]]. * [[File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] would display an image, but [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] will produce a link to [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]]. * [[pt:Internet]] will create an [[interlanguage link]], but [[:pt:Internet]] will produce an interwiki link to [[:pt:Internet]] (in the Portuguese Wikipedia). * [[//Hus]] will produce an (invalid) external link wrapped in brackets ("[[//Hus]]"), but [[://Hus]] will produce a link to [[://Hus]]. * [[Computer]] links to the [[Computer]] article; adding a colon like [[:Computer]] can be done, but the colon is unneeded and makes no difference. ==With templates== A colon can also be used to transclude a page in mainspace, rather than template space. For example, {{tlx|The Beatles}} will transclude [[Template:The Beatles]], but {{[[The Beatles|:The Beatles]]}} will transclude the mainspace article [[The Beatles]]. This is useful when the mainspace article has been specially formatted to be transcluded in other articles, by using <noinclude> or <includeonly> tags to indicate the portion that should be included when the article is transcluded. ==With edit summaries== Normally, linking to a [[Wikipedia:User pages|user page]] in an [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] sends a notification to the user. A colon can be used to escape this behaviour. For example, the edit summary "corrected typo by [[User:Example]]" will ping [[User:Example]], but "corrected typo by [[:User:Example]]" will not. {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia link help|Colon trick]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia08]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia13]] [[en:Colon trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia13]] [[en:Colon trick]]

A colon can be used to escape this behaviour. This is useful when the mainspace article has been specially formatted to be transcluded in other articles, by using <noinclude> or <includeonly> tags to indicate the portion that should be included when the article is transcluded. ==With edit summaries== Normally, linking to a [[Wikipedia:User pages|user page]] in an [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] sends a notification to the user. A colon can be used to escape this behaviour. For example, the edit summary "corrected typo by [[User:Example]]" will ping [[User:Example]], but "corrected typo by [[:User:Example]]" will not. {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia link help|Colon trick]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Colon trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[en:Colon trick]]

By adding a [[Colon (punctuation)|colon]] just inside the opening double square brackets of the link, the default behaviour of the [[MediaWiki software]] will be bypassed. It will appear as a conventional [[Help:Link|wikilink]]. ==Examples== * [[Category:Help]] would list this page in Category:Help, but [[:Category:Help]] will produce a link to [[:Category:Help]]. * [[File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] would display an image, but [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] will produce a link to [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]]. * [[pt:Internet]] will create an [[interlanguage link]], but [[:pt:Internet]] will produce an interwiki link to [[:pt:Internet]] (in the Portuguese Wikipedia). * [[//Hus]] will produce an (invalid) external link wrapped in brackets ("[[//Hus]]"), but [[://Hus]] will produce a link to [[://Hus]]. * [[Computer]] links to the [[Computer]] article; adding a colon like [[:Computer]] can be done, but the colon is unneeded and makes no difference. ==With templates== A colon can also be used to transclude a page in mainspace, rather than template space. For example, {{tlx|The Beatles}} will transclude [[Template:The Beatles]], but {{[[The Beatles|:The Beatles]]}} will transclude the mainspace article [[The Beatles]]. This is useful when the mainspace article has been specially formatted to be transcluded in other articles, by using <noinclude> or <includeonly> tags to indicate the portion that should be included when the article is transcluded. ==With edit summaries== Normally, linking to a [[Wikipedia:User pages|user page]] in an [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] sends a notification to the user. A colon can be used to escape this behaviour. For example, the edit summary "corrected typo by [[User:Example]]" will ping [[User:Example]], but "corrected typo by [[:User:Example]]" will not. {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia link help|Colon trick]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Colon trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[en:Colon trick]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

This is useful when the mainspace article has been specially formatted to be transcluded in other articles, by using <noinclude> or <includeonly> tags to indicate the portion that should be included when the article is transcluded. ==With edit summaries== Normally, linking to a [[Wikipedia:User pages|user page]] in an [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] sends a notification to the user. A colon can be used to escape this behaviour. For example, the edit summary "corrected typo by [[User:Example]]" will ping [[User:Example]], but "corrected typo by [[:User:Example]]" will not. {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia link help|Colon trick]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Colon trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[en:Colon trick]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{For|the style guideline about using colon in articles|MOS:COLON}} {{Wikipedia how-to|WP:COLON|WP:COLONTRICK|H::}} {{Linking and page manipulation|linking and diffs}} The '''colon trick''' is a method of providing a link to a [[Wikipedia:Category|Category]], [[Help:Images|image]] or [[Help:Interwiki linking|interwiki link]] without adding the page to the Category, displaying the image or adding the interwiki link to the [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage links]]. It also allows for linking to pages with titles beginning with a recognized external link prefix, such as [[:http://]] or [[://Hus]]. By adding a [[Colon (punctuation)|colon]] just inside the opening double square brackets of the link, the default behaviour of the [[MediaWiki software]] will be bypassed. It will appear as a conventional [[Help:Link|wikilink]]. ==Examples== * [[Category:Help]] would list this page in Category:Help, but [[:Category:Help]] will produce a link to [[:Category:Help]]. * [[File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] would display an image, but [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] will produce a link to [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]]. * [[pt:Internet]] will create an [[interlanguage link]], but [[:pt:Internet]] will produce an interwiki link to [[:pt:Internet]] (in the Portuguese Wikipedia). * [[//Hus]] will produce an (invalid) external link wrapped in brackets ("[[//Hus]]"), but [[://Hus]] will produce a link to [[://Hus]]. * [[Computer]] links to the [[Computer]] article; adding a colon like [[:Computer]] can be done, but the colon is unneeded and makes no difference. ==With templates== A colon can also be used to transclude a page in mainspace, rather than template space. For example, {{tlx|The Beatles}} will transclude [[Template:The Beatles]], but {{[[The Beatles|:The Beatles]]}} will transclude the mainspace article [[The Beatles]]. This is useful when the mainspace article has been specially formatted to be transcluded in other articles, by using <noinclude> or <includeonly> tags to indicate the portion that should be included when the article is transcluded. ==With edit summaries== Normally, linking to a [[Wikipedia:User pages|user page]] in an [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] sends a notification to the user. A colon can be used to escape this behaviour. For example, the edit summary "corrected typo by [[User:Example]]" will ping [[User:Example]], but "corrected typo by [[:User:Example]]" will not. {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia link help|Colon trick]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Colon trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Colon trick]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Colon trick]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

It will appear as a conventional [[Help:Link|wikilink]]. ==Examples== * [[Category:Help]] would list this page in Category:Help, but [[:Category:Help]] will produce a link to [[:Category:Help]]. * [[File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] would display an image, but [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]] will produce a link to [[:File:Wikipedia-logo.png]]. * [[pt:Internet]] will create an [[interlanguage link]], but [[:pt:Internet]] will produce an interwiki link to [[:pt:Internet]] (in the Portuguese Wikipedia). * [[//Hus]] will produce an (invalid) external link wrapped in brackets ("[[//Hus]]"), but [[://Hus]] will produce a link to [[://Hus]]. * [[Computer]] links to the [[Computer]] article; adding a colon like [[:Computer]] can be done, but the colon is unneeded and makes no difference. ==With templates== A colon can also be used to transclude a page in mainspace, rather than template space. For example, {{tlx|The Beatles}} will transclude [[Template:The Beatles]], but {{[[The Beatles|:The Beatles]]}} will transclude the mainspace article [[The Beatles]]. This is useful when the mainspace article has been specially formatted to be transcluded in other articles, by using <noinclude> or <includeonly> tags to indicate the portion that should be included when the article is transcluded. ==With edit summaries== Normally, linking to a [[Wikipedia:User pages|user page]] in an [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] sends a notification to the user. A colon can be used to escape this behaviour. For example, the edit summary "corrected typo by [[User:Example]]" will ping [[User:Example]], but "corrected typo by [[:User:Example]]" will not. {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:Wikipedia link help|Colon trick]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Colon trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Colon trick]][[en:Colon trick]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Colon trick]][[en:Colon trick]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Colon trick]][[Category:11]][[sn:Colon trick]][[Category:11]]
